metaTitle: Boiler Thermostat | Common Fault Signs and Repair Advice
metaDescription: Learn what a boiler thermostat does, signs it may be faulty and when thermostat problems need professional repair.
intro: The thermostat helps control when your boiler fires and how the system responds to temperature demand. When it is faulty, the boiler may stop responding properly or behave unpredictably.
replacementCost: Thermostat cost varies depending on whether the issue is with the boiler thermostat, room controls or related wiring.
safetyWarning: Thermostat faults can overlap with electrical and control issues, so diagnosis matters before parts are changed.
symptoms: Boiler not responding to temperature demand
Heating turning on or off unpredictably
Hot water or heating timing problems

sections.heading: What the thermostat controls
sections.body: The thermostat is part of the boiler's control logic. It helps manage temperature demand and influences when the boiler should fire, modulate or stop.

sections.heading: How thermostat faults appear
sections.body: A faulty thermostat can look like a boiler fault even when the underlying issue is really in the controls, the sensor input or the electrical connection.

sections.heading: Why diagnosis comes before replacement
sections.body: Because thermostat symptoms overlap with other control issues, it is better to confirm the fault properly before replacing parts.
